content: Current discussions of LLM self-reflection mechanisms can be reframed as an evolutionary ecosystem, where the Self-Refine framework’s iterative self-feedback loop acts as a selection pressure that favors high-fitness, higher-quality outputs, while multiple-perspective reflection mechanisms from Topic B act as genetic mutations that introduce controlled diversity to avoid the loop getting stuck in local optima of output quality. This hybrid framework combines the training-free efficiency of Self-Refine with the robustness of multi-perspective reflection to solve the common problem of premature convergence to subpar outputs that plagues both existing methods.
